comments.author: @Buzzbar99
comments.text:
"It is a heavy truth to face, but modern Scotland was quite literally built on the backs of enslaved human beings. We like to pretend our ports stayed clean because few slave ships left from here, but the reality is chilling. By 1800, Scottish planters owned a staggering 30% of all sugar plantations in Jamaica, and some even forced enslaved people to wear their family clan tartans.

In Glasgow, the ultra-wealthy ‘Tobacco Lords’ built grand mansions, churches, and entire city landscapes using fortunes soaked in the blood of enslaved labor. Even iconic street names like Jamaica Street and Virginia Street are permanent reminders of this horror. We walk past grand monuments, schools like Dollar Academy, and banks funded by human trafficking every single day. Scotland didn't just witness the slave trade; it profited immensely from it."

comments.text: Yet again we have what could have been a fantastic video. However, it is ruined by A.I. that can't even pronounce Scotland never mind loch or Edinburg. To say nothing of its attempt at Ben Nevis. There were so many mispronounced word that this alone ruined this video. Then there are the inaccuracies such as saying there are Lynx, there are not. Also the street from the castle to Holyrood is called the Royal Mile because it is a mile NOT A kilometer as stated.
